preamble.summary: Numbers in the SP contains a moderate number of significant variants, primarily in the Balaam oracles (Num 22-24), census figures, and the itinerary sections. SP continues its harmonistic program by inserting fulfillment reports where MT has only commands, and it makes several adjustments to the Balaam narrative that affect theological interpretation. Some census numbers differ between SP and MT.
preamble.notable_variants: The Balaam oracles (Num 23-24) contain variants that affect messianic interpretation. Numbers 10:10 has a Gerizim-related variant. Several census figures in chapters 1-4 and 26 show minor numerical differences. The red heifer legislation (Num 19) has minor terminological variants.
preamble.theological_significance: SP Numbers reinforces Gerizim theology in the few places where sanctuary location is relevant. The Balaam oracles are interpreted in Samaritan tradition as referring to the Taheb (Samaritan restorer figure) rather than a Davidic king. The harmonistic expansions continue SP's program of ensuring narrative consistency between divine command and human execution.

variants.mt_rendering: forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty
variants.sp_rendering: forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty
notes: Most first-census figures agree between MT and SP. Minor orthographic differences in how numbers are expressed.

variants.mt_rendering: Reuel
variants.sp_rendering: Deuel
notes: The name of the Gadite leader's father varies: MT reads 'Reuel' here (but 'Deuel' in 1:14, 7:42, 10:20). SP reads 'Deuel' consistently throughout, harmonizing the name. The resh/daleth confusion is one of the most common scribal errors in Hebrew.

variants.mt_rendering: (Standard vessel list)
variants.sp_rendering: (Expanded with additional items from parallel passages)
notes: SP harmonizes the Kohathite duties with related passages, adding minor details about the service vessels.

variants.mt_rendering: The LORD lift up his countenance upon you
variants.sp_rendering: The LORD lift up his countenance upon you
notes: The priestly blessing (Birkat Kohanim) is identical in MT and SP. This blessing remains central to Samaritan liturgy.

variants.sp_reading: Trumpet legislation with added reference to the chosen place
variants.mt_rendering: (Standard legislation about festival trumpets)
variants.sp_rendering: (SP adds contextual reference to worship at the chosen place)
notes: SP connects the trumpet legislation to the designated sanctuary, reinforcing centralized worship at Gerizim.

variants.mt_rendering: three days
variants.sp_rendering: three days
notes: The ark's journey from Sinai — text agrees between MT and SP.

variants.mt_rendering: the Cushite woman
variants.sp_rendering: the Cushite woman
notes: SP agrees with MT on the Cushite wife reference. Some SP manuscripts show minor orthographic variance, but the content is identical. Samaritan tradition does not identify this woman differently from Jewish tradition.

variants.mt_rendering: and he beholds the form of the LORD
variants.sp_rendering: and he beholds the form of the LORD
notes: SP retains the anthropomorphic language here despite its usual tendency to soften such expressions. Moses' unique prophetic status is affirmed in both traditions.

variants.mt_rendering: And the LORD spoke to Moses saying
variants.sp_rendering: (SP adds the people's request to send spies, harmonizing with Deut 1:22-23)
notes: In MT, the spy mission originates with God's command. Deuteronomy 1:22 says the people requested it. SP harmonizes by inserting the people's request before God's command, making both accounts consistent. This is one of SP's most notable harmonistic additions in Numbers.

variants.mt_rendering: Here we are, and we will go up
variants.sp_rendering: Here we are, and we will go up
notes: The failed Hormah campaign narrative agrees between MT and SP.

variants.mt_rendering: And Korah took
variants.sp_rendering: And Korah took
notes: The Korah rebellion narrative is essentially identical in MT and SP. No significant theological variants.

variants.mt_rendering: a red heifer, unblemished
variants.sp_rendering: a red heifer, unblemished
notes: The red heifer legislation is identical in MT and SP. Both traditions preserve this ritual, though Samaritans last performed it on Mount Gerizim.

variants.mt_rendering: These are the waters of Meribah
variants.sp_rendering: These are the waters of Meribah
notes: The Meribah narrative agrees between MT and SP.

variants.sp_reading: Itinerary stations with minor name variants
variants.mt_rendering: (Standard itinerary)
variants.sp_rendering: (Minor orthographic variants in place names)
notes: Several place names in the wilderness itinerary show minor spelling differences between MT and SP.

variants.sp_reading: פתורה אשר על הנהר ארץ בני עמון
variants.mt_rendering: to Pethor, which is by the River, the land of the sons of his people
variants.sp_rendering: to Pethor, which is by the River, the land of the sons of Ammon
notes: SP reads 'Ammon' (עמון) where MT reads 'his people' (עמו). SP's reading gives a more specific geographical location and is supported by some LXX manuscripts.

variants.sp_reading: SP expands God's speech to Balaam
variants.mt_rendering: If the men have come to call you
variants.sp_rendering: (SP expands the divine speech with additional conditions)
notes: SP adds material to God's nighttime speech to Balaam, smoothing the apparent contradiction between God giving permission (22:20) and being angry that Balaam went (22:22).

variants.mt_rendering: Who can count the dust of Jacob
variants.sp_rendering: Who can count the dust of Jacob
notes: Balaam's first oracle largely agrees between MT and SP. Minor orthographic differences only.

variants.mt_rendering: And his king shall be higher than Agag
variants.sp_rendering: And his king shall be higher than Gog
notes: SP reads 'Gog' (גוג) where MT reads 'Agag' (אגג). The LXX also reads 'Gog.' This variant has eschatological implications: 'Agag' connects to the historical Amalekite king, while 'Gog' connects to the apocalyptic enemy of Ezekiel 38-39. SP's reading may be original or may reflect later eschatological interests.

variants.mt_reading: דָּרַךְ כּוֹכָב מִיַּעֲקֹב וְקָם שֵׁבֶט מִיִּשְׂרָאֵל
variants.sp_reading: דרך כוכב מיעקב וקם שבט מישראל
variants.mt_rendering: A star shall come out of Jacob, and a scepter shall rise out of Israel
variants.sp_rendering: A star shall come out of Jacob, and a scepter shall rise out of Israel
notes: The star prophecy agrees in wording between MT and SP, but interpretation differs dramatically. Jewish tradition reads this as a royal/messianic prophecy (applied to David or the Messiah). Samaritan tradition reads it as referring to the Taheb, the restorer figure who will re-establish true worship at Gerizim.

variants.mt_rendering: (Census instructions)
variants.sp_rendering: (SP adds harmonistic detail from Num 1)
notes: SP harmonizes the second census introduction with the first census (Num 1), adding parallel formulaic language.

variants.mt_rendering: the waters of Meribah of Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin
variants.sp_rendering: the waters of Meribah of Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin
notes: Moses' punishment narrative agrees between MT and SP.

variants.sp_reading: Expanded Joshua commissioning with additional charge
variants.mt_rendering: (Moses lays hands on Joshua)
variants.sp_rendering: (SP adds material from Deut 3:21-22 to Joshua's commissioning)
notes: SP imports Moses' charge to Joshua from Deuteronomy 3:21-22 into this passage, harmonizing the two accounts. This is a significant structural expansion.

variants.mt_rendering: (Full itinerary list)
variants.sp_rendering: (Same stations with orthographic name variants)
notes: The wilderness itinerary in Numbers 33 is substantially the same in MT and SP. Several station names show minor spelling differences.

variants.sp_reading: Levitical city measurements — minor numerical variant
variants.mt_rendering: (2,000 cubits on each side)
variants.sp_rendering: (Same measurement with minor expression variant)
notes: Minor difference in how the Levitical city measurements are expressed. The actual dimensions are identical.
